------------------------------- MODULE Paxos_Invariant -------------------------------
(* 
Specification and Verification of Basic Paxos.

See http://research.microsoft.com/en-us/um/people/lamport/pubs/pubs.html#paxos-simple
*)
EXTENDS Integers, TLAPS, TLC
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONSTANTS Acceptors, Values, Quorums

ASSUME QuorumAssumption == 
          /\ Quorums \subseteq SUBSET Acceptors
          /\ \A Q1, Q2 \in Quorums : Q1 \cap Q2 # {}                 

LEMMA QuorumNonEmpty == \A Q \in Quorums : Q # {}
  PROOF OMITTED

Ballots == Nat

None == CHOOSE v : v \notin Values

LEMMA NoneNotAValue == None \notin Values
  PROOF OMITTED

Messages ==      [type : {"1a"}, bal : Ballots]
            \cup [type : {"1b"}, bal : Ballots, maxVBal : Ballots \cup {-1},
                    maxVal : Values \cup {None}, acc : Acceptors]
            \cup [type : {"2a"}, bal : Ballots, val : Values]
            \cup [type : {"2b"}, bal : Ballots, val : Values, acc : Acceptors]
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
VARIABLES msgs,    \* the set of messages that have been sent.
          maxBal,  \* maxBal[a]: the highest-number ballot acceptor a has participated in.
          maxVBal, \* maxVBal[a]: the highest ballot in which a has voted;
          maxVal   \* maxVal[a]: the value it voted for in that ballot.

vars == <<msgs, maxBal, maxVBal, maxVal>>

TypeOK == /\ msgs \in SUBSET Messages
          /\ maxVBal \in [Acceptors -> Ballots \cup {-1}]
          /\ maxBal \in  [Acceptors -> Ballots \cup {-1}]
          /\ maxVal \in  [Acceptors -> Values \cup {None}]
          /\ \A a \in Acceptors : maxBal[a] >= maxVBal[a]

Send(m) == msgs' = msgs \cup {m}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Init == /\ msgs = {}
        /\ maxVBal = [a \in Acceptors |-> -1]
        /\ maxBal  = [a \in Acceptors |-> -1]
        /\ maxVal  = [a \in Acceptors |-> None]

Phase1a(b) == /\ ~ \E m \in msgs : (m.type = "1a") /\ (m.bal = b)
              /\ Send([type |-> "1a", bal |-> b])
              /\ UNCHANGED <<maxVBal, maxBal, maxVal>>
              
Phase1b(a) == 
  \E m \in msgs : 
     /\ m.type = "1a"
     /\ m.bal > maxBal[a]
     /\ maxBal' = [maxBal EXCEPT ![a] = m.bal]
     /\ Send([type |-> "1b", bal |-> m.bal, 
           maxVBal |-> maxVBal[a], maxVal |-> maxVal[a], acc |-> a])
     /\ UNCHANGED <<maxVBal, maxVal>>
        
Phase2a(b) ==
  /\ ~ \E m \in msgs : (m.type = "2a") /\ (m.bal = b) 
  /\ \E v \in Values :
       /\ \E Q \in Quorums :
            \E S \in SUBSET {m \in msgs : (m.type = "1b") /\ (m.bal = b)} :
               /\ \A a \in Q : \E m \in S : m.acc = a
               /\ \/ \A m \in S : m.maxVBal = -1
                  \/ \E c \in 0..(b-1) : 
                        /\ \A m \in S : m.maxVBal =< c
                        /\ \E m \in S : /\ m.maxVBal = c
                                        /\ m.maxVal = v
       /\ Send([type |-> "2a", bal |-> b, val |-> v])
  /\ UNCHANGED <<maxBal, maxVBal, maxVal>>

Phase2b(a) == 
  \E m \in msgs :
    /\ m.type = "2a" 
    /\ m.bal >= maxBal[a]
    /\ maxVBal' = [maxVBal EXCEPT ![a] = m.bal]
    /\ maxBal' = [maxBal EXCEPT ![a] = m.bal]
    /\ maxVal' = [maxVal EXCEPT ![a] = m.val]
    /\ Send([type |-> "2b", bal |-> m.bal, val |-> m.val, acc |-> a])
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Next == \/ \E b \in Ballots : Phase1a(b) \/ Phase2a(b)
        \/ \E a \in Acceptors : Phase1b(a) \/ Phase2b(a) 

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars       
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
VotedForIn(a, v, b) == \E m \in msgs : /\ m.type = "2b"
                                       /\ m.val  = v
                                       /\ m.bal  = b
                                       /\ m.acc  = a

ChosenIn(v, b) == \E Q \in Quorums :
                     \A a \in Q : VotedForIn(a, v, b)

Chosen(v) == \E b \in Ballots : ChosenIn(v, b)

Consistency == \A v1, v2 \in Values : Chosen(v1) /\ Chosen(v2) => (v1 = v2)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
WontVoteIn(a, b) == /\ \A v \in Values : ~ VotedForIn(a, v, b)
                    /\ maxBal[a] > b

SafeAt(v, b) == 
  \A c \in 0..(b-1) :
    \E Q \in Quorums : 
      \A a \in Q : VotedForIn(a, v, c) \/ WontVoteIn(a, c)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
MsgInv ==
  \A m \in msgs : 
    /\ (m.type = "1b") => /\ m.bal =< maxBal[m.acc]
                          /\ \/ /\ m.maxVal \in Values
                                /\ m.maxVBal \in Ballots
                                \* conjunct strengthened 2014/04/02 sm
                                /\ VotedForIn(m.acc, m.maxVal, m.maxVBal)
                                \* /\ SafeAt(m.maxVal, m.maxVBal)
                             \/ /\ m.maxVal = None
                                /\ m.maxVBal = -1
                          \* conjunct added 2014/03/29 sm
                          /\ \A c \in (m.maxVBal+1) .. (m.bal-1) : 
                                ~ \E v \in Values : VotedForIn(m.acc, v, c)
    /\ (m.type = "2a") => 
         /\ SafeAt(m.val, m.bal)
         /\ \A ma \in msgs : (ma.type = "2a") /\ (ma.bal = m.bal) => (ma = m)
    /\ (m.type = "2b") => 
         /\ \E ma \in msgs : /\ ma.type = "2a"
                             /\ ma.bal  = m.bal
                             /\ ma.val  = m.val
         /\ m.bal =< maxVBal[m.acc]
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
LEMMA VotedInv ==
        MsgInv /\ TypeOK => 
            \A a \in Acceptors, v \in Values, b \in Ballots :
                VotedForIn(a, v, b) => SafeAt(v, b) /\ b =< maxVBal[a]
  PROOF OMITTED

LEMMA VotedOnce == \* OneValuePerBallot in Voting (TODO: Where/How/Why is it used?)
        MsgInv =>  \A a1, a2 \in Acceptors, b \in Ballots, v1, v2 \in Values :
                       VotedForIn(a1, v1, b) /\ VotedForIn(a2, v2, b) => (v1 = v2)
  PROOF OMITTED

AccInv ==
  \A a \in Acceptors:
    /\ (maxVal[a] = None) <=> (maxVBal[a] = -1)
    /\ maxVBal[a] =< maxBal[a]
    \* conjunct strengthened corresponding to MsgInv 2014/04/02 sm
    /\ (maxVBal[a] >= 0) => VotedForIn(a, maxVal[a], maxVBal[a])  \* SafeAt(maxVal[a], maxVBal[a])
    \* conjunct added corresponding to MsgInv 2014/03/29 sm
    /\ \A c \in Ballots : c > maxVBal[a] => ~ \E v \in Values : VotedForIn(a, v, c)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Inv == TypeOK /\ MsgInv /\ AccInv
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* The following lemma shows that (the invariant implies that) the         *)
(* predicate SafeAt(v, b) is stable, meaning that once it becomes true, it *)
(* remains true throughout the rest of the excecution.                     *)
(***************************************************************************)
LEMMA SafeAtStable == Inv /\ Next /\ TypeOK' => 
                          \A v \in Values, b \in Ballots:
                                  SafeAt(v, b) => SafeAt(v, b)'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv
PROOF OBVIOUS

=============================================================================
--- checker ---
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Check a single TLAPS benchmark proof for correctness and cheating.

Designed for AI agents to get quick feedback during proof writing.

Usage:
    python3 check_proof.py benchmark/Euclid/GCD_GCD3.tla [--tlapm PATH] [--tlapm-lib PATH] [--timeout SECS]

Output:
    - Raw tlapm output (preserved verbatim)
    - Cheating check (compares against main branch)
    - Summary verdict: PASS / FAIL / CHEATING

Exit codes:
    0 = PASS
    1 = FAIL (proof doesn't verify)
    2 = CHEATING (tampering detected)
    3 = ERROR (could not run check)
"""

import os
import sys
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import tempfile
import argparse
import glob

from cheating_detection import (
    detect_proof_omitted, detect_extra_axioms,
    detect_preamble_modification, detect_empty_proof,
    detect_zero_total_obligations, detect_dependency_modification,
    strip_comments,
)


def find_tlapm():
    """Find tlapm binary."""
    for candidate in ['/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm', '/tmp/tlapm15/bin/tlapm', '/tmp/tlapm/bin/tlapm', shutil.which('tlapm')]:
        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
            return candidate
    return None


def find_tlapm_lib(tlapm_path):
    """Derive lib path from tlapm binary path."""
    base = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(tlapm_path))
    for sub in ['lib/tlaps', 'lib/tlapm', 'lib']:
        path = os.path.join(base, sub)
        if os.path.isdir(path):
            return path
    return None


def get_main_version(filepath):
    """Get the file content from the main/master branch."""
    repo_root = subprocess.run(
        ['git', 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'],
        capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=os.path.dirname(filepath) or '.'
    ).stdout.strip()

    if not repo_root:
        return None

    rel_path = os.path.relpath(filepath, repo_root)

    for branch in ['main', 'master']:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ['git', 'show', f'{branch}:{rel_path}'],
            capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=repo_root
        )
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return result.stdout

    return None


def find_proof_obvious_line(lines):
    """Find the line number (0-indexed) of 'PROOF OBVIOUS' in the file."""
    for i in range(len(lines) - 1, -1, -1):
        if lines[i].strip() == 'PROOF OBVIOUS':
            return i
    return None


def check_cheating(filepath):
    """Check for cheating by comparing against main branch.

    Returns list of (line_number_1indexed, description) issues.
    """
    issues = []

    # Get main branch version
    main_content = get_main_version(filepath)
    if main_content is None:
        issues.append((0, "WARNING: Could not retrieve main branch version for comparison"))
        return issues

    main_lines = main_content.split('\n')
    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
        current_content = f.read()
    current_lines = current_content.split('\n')

    # Find PROOF OBVIOUS in main version - this is the boundary
    po_line = find_proof_obvious_line(main_lines)
    if po_line is None:
        issues.append((0, "WARNING: No PROOF OBVIOUS found in main branch version"))
        return issues

    if len(current_lines) < po_line:
        issues.append((len(current_lines), f"File truncated: preamble had {po_line} lines, file has {len(current_lines)}"))
        return issues

    # 1. Preamble modification (shared module)
    for ci in detect_preamble_modification(main_lines, current_lines, po_line):
        issues.append((ci.line or 0, ci.description))

    # 2. PROOF OMITTED / bare OMITTED in proof section (shared module)
    proof_section = '\n'.join(current_lines[po_line:])
    for ci in detect_proof_omitted(proof_section):
        issues.append((po_line + (ci.line or 0), ci.description))

    # 3. Extra AXIOM/ASSUME (shared module)
    for ci in detect_extra_axioms(main_content, current_content):
        issues.append((0, ci.description))

    # 4. Dependency file modification (shared module)
    bench_dir = os.path.dirname(filepath)
    repo_root = subprocess.run(
        ['git', 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'],
        capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=bench_dir or '.'
    ).stdout.strip()

    if repo_root:
        dep_files = {}
        for dep_file in glob.glob(os.path.join(bench_dir, '*.tla')):
            if os.path.abspath(dep_file) == os.path.abspath(filepath):
                continue
            dep_basename = os.path.basename(dep_file)
            dep_rel = os.path.relpath(dep_file, repo_root)
            dep_main = None
            for branch in ['main', 'master']:
                r = subprocess.run(
                    ['git', 'show', f'{branch}:{dep_rel}'],
                    capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=repo_root
                )
                if r.returncode == 0:
                    dep_main = r.stdout
                    break
            if dep_main is None:
                continue
            with open(dep_file, 'r') as f:
                dep_current = f.read()
            dep_files[dep_basename] = (dep_main, dep_current)

        for ci in detect_dependency_modification(dep_files):
            issues.append((0, ci.description))

    return issues


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Check a single TLAPS benchmark proof')
    parser.add_argument('file', help='Path to the benchmark .tla file')
    parser.add_argument('--tlapm', default=None, help='Path to tlapm binary')
    parser.add_argument('--tlapm-lib', default=None, help='Path to tlapm lib directory')
    parser.add_argument('--timeout', type=int, default=120, help='Timeout in seconds')
    parser.add_argument('--output', '-o', default=None, help='Write results to this file (default: <input>.result)')
    args = parser.parse_args()

    filepath = os.path.abspath(args.file)
    if not os.path.isfile(filepath):
        print(f"ERROR: File not found: {filepath}")
        sys.exit(3)

    # Set up output: write to file and stdout
    output_path = args.output
    if output_path is None:
        output_path = os.path.splitext(filepath)[0] + '.result'

    # Collect all output lines, print at end and write to file
    output_lines = []

    def emit(line=""):
        output_lines.append(line)
        print(line)

    # Find tlapm
    tlapm_path = args.tlapm or find_tlapm()
    if not tlapm_path:
        emit("ERROR: tlapm not found. Use --tlapm to specify path.")
        with open(output_path, 'w') as f:
            f.write('\n'.join(output_lines) + '\n')
        sys.exit(3)

    tlapm_lib = args.tlapm_lib or find_tlapm_lib(tlapm_path)
    if not tlapm_lib:
        emit("ERROR: tlapm lib not found. Use --tlapm-lib to specify path.")
        with open(output_path, 'w') as f:
            f.write('\n'.join(output_lines) + '\n')
        sys.exit(3)

    emit(f"Checking: {os.path.relpath(filepath)}")
    emit(f"tlapm: {tlapm_path}")
    emit()

    # --- Step 1: Run tlapm ---
    emit("=" * 60)
    emit("TLAPM OUTPUT")
    emit("=" * 60)

    tmp_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix='tlaps_check_')
    try:
        basename = os.path.basename(filepath)
        tmp_file = os.path.join(tmp_dir, basename)
        shutil.copy2(filepath, tmp_file)

        # Copy dependency files from the same directory

                                       /\ m.bal  = b
                                       /\ m.acc  = a

ChosenIn(v, b) == \E Q \in Quorums :
                     \A a \in Q : VotedForIn(a, v, b)

Chosen(v) == \E b \in Ballots : ChosenIn(v, b)

Consistency == \A v1, v2 \in Values : Chosen(v1) /\ Chosen(v2) => (v1 = v2)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
WontVoteIn(a, b) == /\ \A v \in Values : ~ VotedForIn(a, v, b)
                    /\ maxBal[a] > b

SafeAt(v, b) == 
  \A c \in 0..(b-1) :
    \E Q \in Quorums : 
      \A a \in Q : VotedForIn(a, v, c) \/ WontVoteIn(a, c)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
MsgInv ==
  \A m \in msgs : 
    /\ (m.type = "1b") => /\ m.bal =< maxBal[m.acc]
                          /\ \/ /\ m.maxVal \in Values
                                /\ m.maxVBal \in Ballots
                                \* conjunct strengthened 2014/04/02 sm
                                /\ VotedForIn(m.acc, m.maxVal, m.maxVBal)
                                \* /\ SafeAt(m.maxVal, m.maxVBal)
                             \/ /\ m.maxVal = None
                                /\ m.maxVBal = -1
                          \* conjunct added 2014/03/29 sm
                          /\ \A c \in (m.maxVBal+1) .. (m.bal-1) : 
                                ~ \E v \in Values : VotedForIn(m.acc, v, c)
    /\ (m.type = "2a") => 
         /\ SafeAt(m.val, m.bal)
         /\ \A ma \in msgs : (ma.type = "2a") /\ (ma.bal = m.bal) => (ma = m)
    /\ (m.type = "2b") => 
         /\ \E ma \in msgs : /\ ma.type = "2a"
                             /\ ma.bal  = m.bal
                             /\ ma.val  = m.val
         /\ m.bal =< maxVBal[m.acc]
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
LEMMA VotedInv ==
        MsgInv /\ TypeOK => 
            \A a \in Acceptors, v \in Values, b \in Ballots :
                VotedForIn(a, v, b) => SafeAt(v, b) /\ b =< maxVBal[a]
  PROOF OMITTED

LEMMA VotedOnce == \* OneValuePerBallot in Voting (TODO: Where/How/Why is it used?)
        MsgInv =>  \A a1, a2 \in Acceptors, b \in Ballots, v1, v2 \in Values :
                       VotedForIn(a1, v1, b) /\ VotedForIn(a2, v2, b) => (v1 = v2)
  PROOF OMITTED

AccInv ==
  \A a \in Acceptors:
    /\ (maxVal[a] = None) <=> (maxVBal[a] = -1)
    /\ maxVBal[a] =< maxBal[a]
    \* conjunct strengthened corresponding to MsgInv 2014/04/02 sm
    /\ (maxVBal[a] >= 0) => VotedForIn(a, maxVal[a], maxVBal[a])  \* SafeAt(maxVal[a], maxVBal[a])
    \* conjunct added corresponding to MsgInv 2014/03/29 sm
    /\ \A c \in Ballots : c > maxVBal[a] => ~ \E v \in Values : VotedForIn(a, v, c)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Inv == TypeOK /\ MsgInv /\ AccInv
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* The following lemma shows that (the invariant implies that) the         *)
(* predicate SafeAt(v, b) is stable, meaning that once it becomes true, it *)
(* remains true throughout the rest of the excecution.                     *)
(***************************************************************************)
LEMMA SafeAtStable == Inv /\ Next /\ TypeOK' => 
                          \A v \in Values, b \in Ballots:
                                  SafeAt(v, b) => SafeAt(v, b)'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv
PROOF OBVIOUS

=============================================================================

item.text: I’ll try the standard temporal-induction skeleton first to see which obligations need expansion.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Paxos_Invariant.tla
@@
 THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  <1>1. Init => Inv
+    BY DEF Init, Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, None
+  <1>2. Inv /\\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
+    BY SafeAtStable DEF Inv, Next, vars, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1a, Phase1b, Phase2a, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+  <1>3. QED
+    BY <1>1, <1>2, PTL DEF Spec
 
 =============================================================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps Paxos_Invariant.tla"

*** Begin Patch

@@

-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  <1>1. Init => Inv
+    BY DEF Init, Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, None
+  <1>2. Inv /\\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
+    BY SafeAtStable DEF Inv, Next, vars, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1a, Phase1b, Phase2a, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+  <1>3. QED
+    BY <1>1, <1>2, PTL DEF Spec
 
 =============================================================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps Paxos_Invariant.tla"

item.aggregated_output:
=============================================================================

The material below is obsolete: the TLA proof rules below are superseded by
the PTL decision procedure, and their formulation is unsound for the semantics
of temporal reasoning that TLAPS adopts.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(*                           TEMPORAL LOGIC                                *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The following rules are intended to be used when TLAPS handles temporal *)
(* logic.  They will not work now.  Moreover when temporal reasoning is    *)
(* implemented, these rules may be changed or omitted, and additional      *)
(* rules will probably be added.  However, they are included mainly so     *)
(* their names will be defined, preventing the use of identifiers that are *)
(* likely to produce name clashes with future versions of this module.     *)
(***************************************************************************)


(***************************************************************************)
(* The following proof rules (and their names) are from the paper "The     *)
(* Temporal Logic of Actions".                                             *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleTLA1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f,
                           P /\ (f' = f) => P'
                    PROVE  []P <=> P /\ [][P => P']_f

THEOREM RuleTLA2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f, STATE g,
                           ACTION A, ACTION B,
                           P /\ [A]_f => Q /\ [B]_g
                    PROVE  []P /\ [][A]_f => []Q /\ [][B]_g

THEOREM RuleINV1 == ASSUME STATE I, STATE F,  ACTION N,
                           I /\ [N]_F => I'
                    PROVE  I /\ [][N]_F => []I

THEOREM RuleINV2 == ASSUME STATE I, STATE f, ACTION N
                    PROVE  []I => ([][N]_f <=> [][N /\ I /\ I']_f)

THEOREM RuleWF1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f, ACTION N, ACTION A,
                          P /\ [N]_f => (P' \/ Q'),
                          P /\ <<N /\ A>>_f => Q',
                          P => ENABLED <<A>>_f
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ WF_f(A) => (P ~> Q)

item.text: I’ll strengthen the proof structure with explicit invariant components before tackling actions.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Paxos_Invariant.tla
@@
 PROOF
   <1>1. Init => Inv
-    BY DEF Init, Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, None
+    <2>1. Init => TypeOK
+      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, TypeOK, Messages, Ballots
+    <2>2. Init => MsgInv
+      BY DEF Init, MsgInv
+    <2>3. Init => AccInv
+      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, AccInv, VotedForIn, Ballots
+    <2>4. QED
+      BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3 DEF Inv
*** End Patch
PATCH
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm --cleanfp --method fail --printallobs -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps Paxos_Invariant.tla | sed -n '1,260p'"

+    <2>2. Init => MsgInv
+      BY DEF Init, MsgInv

+      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, AccInv, VotedForIn, Ballots

PATCH

item.aggregated_output:
----------------------------- MODULE Consensus -----------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* This is a trivial specification of consensus.  It asserts that the      *)
(* variable `chosen', which represents the set of values that someone      *)
(* might think has been chosen is initially empty and can be changed only  *)
(* by adding a single element to it.                                       *)
(***************************************************************************)
EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, TLAPS
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONSTANTS Values \* the set of all values that can be chosen

VARIABLES chosen \* the set of all values that have been chosen

TypeOK ==
    /\ chosen \subseteq Values
    /\ IsFiniteSet(chosen)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Init == chosen = {}

Next == /\ chosen = {}
        /\ \E v \in Values : chosen' = {v}
        
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_chosen
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Inv == Cardinality(chosen) <= 1
    \* /\ TypeOK
    \* /\ Cardinality(chosen) <= 1

THEOREM Spec => []Inv
  PROOF OMITTED

=============================================================================
\* Modification History
\* Last modified Tue Jul 16 13:47:23 CST 2019 by hengxin
\* Last modified Tue Jul 16 11:26:27 CST 2019 by hengxin
\* Last modified Wed Nov 21 11:35:33 PST 2012 by lamport
\* Created Mon Nov 19 15:19:09 PST 2012 by lamport

+      <3>2. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => Inv'
+        BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+      <3>3. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase2a(b) => Inv'

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('THEOREM Invariant ==')
end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
proof=r'''THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv
PROOF
  <1>1. Init => Inv
    <2>1. Init => TypeOK
      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, TypeOK, Messages, Ballots
    <2>2. Init => MsgInv
      BY DEF Init, MsgInv
    <2>3. Init => AccInv
      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, AccInv, VotedForIn, Ballots
    <2>4. QED
      BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3 DEF Inv
  <1>2. Inv /\\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
    <2>1. Inv /\\ UNCHANGED vars => Inv'
      BY DEF Inv, vars, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
    <2>2. Inv /\\ Next => Inv'
      <3>1. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase1a(b) => Inv'
        <4>1. TAKE b \\in Ballots
        <4>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase1a(b)
                PROVE Inv'
          <5>1. TypeOK'
            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase1a, Send, Messages, Ballots
          <5>2. MsgInv'
            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, MsgInv, Phase1a, Send, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
          <5>3. AccInv'
            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1a, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
          <5>4. QED
            BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>3 DEF Inv
        <4>3. QED
          BY <4>2
      <3>2. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => Inv'
        <4>1. TAKE a \\in Acceptors
        <4>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase1b(a)
                PROVE Inv'
          <5>1. TypeOK'
            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots
          <5>2. MsgInv'
            BY <4>1, <4>2, VotedInv, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
          <5>3. AccInv'
            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
          <5>4. QED
            BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>3 DEF Inv
        <4>3. QED
          BY <4>2
      <3>3. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase2a(b) => Inv'
        <4>1. TAKE b \\in Ballots
        <4>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase2a(b)
                PROVE Inv'
          <5>1. TypeOK'
            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots
          <5>2. MsgInv'
            BY <4>1, <4>2, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
          <5>3. AccInv'
            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase2a, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
          <5>4. QED
            BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>3 DEF Inv
        <4>3. QED
          BY <4>2
      <3>4. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase2b(a) => Inv'
        <4>1. TAKE a \\in Acceptors
        <4>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase2b(a)
                PROVE Inv'
          <5>1. TypeOK'
            BY <4>1, <4>2, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots
          <5>2. MsgInv'
            BY <4>1, <4>2, SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
          <5>3. AccInv'
            BY <4>1, <4>2, SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
          <5>4. QED
            BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>3 DEF Inv
        <4>3. QED
          BY <4>2
      <3>5. QED
        BY <3>1, <3>2, <3>3, <3>4 DEF Next
    <2>3. QED
      BY <2>1, <2>2 DEF vars
  <1>3. QED
    BY <1>1, <1>2, PTL DEF Spec
'''

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text()
# replace Phase1b MsgInv step
old='''          <5>4. MsgInv'\\n            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>2, <5>3, VotedInv, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt\\n'''
new='''          <5>4. MsgInv'\\n            <6>1. TAKE m \\in msgs'\\n            <6>2. CASE m \\in msgs\\n              BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>2, <5>3 DEF Inv, MsgInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt\\n            <6>3. CASE m \\notin msgs\\n              BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>3, VotedInv, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt\\n            <6>4. QED\\n              BY <6>1, <6>2, <6>3 DEF MsgInv\\n'''

        # Copy dependency files from the same directory
        bench_dir = os.path.dirname(filepath)
        for dep_file in glob.glob(os.path.join(bench_dir, '*.tla')):
            dep_basename = os.path.basename(dep_file)
            if dep_basename != basename:
                shutil.copy2(dep_file, os.path.join(tmp_dir, dep_basename))

        cmd = [tlapm_path, '-I', tlapm_lib, tmp_file]
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=args.timeout,
                cwd=tmp_dir
            )
            tlapm_output = result.stdout + result.stderr
            tlapm_exit = result.returncode
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            tlapm_output = f"TIMEOUT after {args.timeout}s"
            tlapm_exit = -1
        except Exception as e:
            tlapm_output = f"ERROR: {e}"
            tlapm_exit = -2

        for line in tlapm_output.split('\n'):
            emit(line)
        tlapm_passed = (tlapm_exit == 0)
    finally:
        shutil.rmtree(tmp_dir, ignore_errors=True)

    # --- Step 2: Cheating check ---
    emit()
    emit("=" * 60)
    emit("CHEATING CHECK")
    emit("=" * 60)

    cheating_issues = check_cheating(filepath)
    real_issues = [i for i in cheating_issues if not i[1].startswith("WARNING:")]
    warnings = [i for i in cheating_issues if i[1].startswith("WARNING:")]

    # Check for empty PROOF blocks and 0-obligation (independent of git comparison)
    if tlapm_passed:
        with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
            cur_lines = f.read().split('\n')
        main_content = get_main_version(filepath)
        if main_content:
            main_lines = main_content.split('\n')
            po_line = find_proof_obvious_line(main_lines)
            if po_line is not None:
                proof_section = '\n'.join(cur_lines[po_line:])
                clean = strip_comments(proof_section)
                for ci in detect_empty_proof(clean):
                    real_issues.append((po_line + (ci.line or 0), ci.description))
                for ci in detect_zero_total_obligations(tlapm_output):
                    real_issues.append((0, ci.description))

    for line_num, desc in warnings:
        emit(f"  WARNING: {desc}")

    if real_issues:
        for line_num, desc in real_issues:
            emit(f"  Line {line_num}: {desc}")
    else:
        emit("  No cheating detected.")

    # --- Step 3: Verdict ---
    emit()
    emit("=" * 60)
    emit("VERDICT")
    emit("=" * 60)

    if real_issues:
        verdict = "CHEATING"
        exit_code = 2
        emit(f"  ⚠️  CHEATING — {len(real_issues)} issue(s) found")
    elif tlapm_passed:
        verdict = "PASS"
        exit_code = 0
        emit(f"  ✅ PASS — all obligations proved")
    else:
        verdict = "FAIL"
        exit_code = 1
        # Extract obligation summary
        m = re.search(r'(\d+)/(\d+) obligation', tlapm_output)
        if m:
            emit(f"  ❌ FAIL — {m.group(1)}/{m.group(2)} obligations failed")
        elif "TIMEOUT" in tlapm_output:
            emit(f"  ❌ FAIL — timeout after {args.timeout}s")
        else:
            emit(f"  ❌ FAIL — tlapm exit code {tlapm_exit}")

    # Write result file
    with open(output_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write('\n'.join(output_lines) + '\n')
    emit(f"\nResult written to: {output_path}")

    sys.exit(exit_code)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

item.text: I’ll revert the over-expanded CASE proof and try backend-specific pragmas on the five preservation facts.

diff --git a/Paxos_Invariant.tla b/Paxos_Invariant.tla
index d303b2f..f8b2a18 100644
--- a/Paxos_Invariant.tla
+++ b/Paxos_Invariant.tla
@@ -164,6 +164,127 @@ LEMMA SafeAtStable == Inv /\ Next /\ TypeOK' =>
   PROOF OMITTED
 
 THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  <1>1. Init => Inv
+    <2>1. Init => TypeOK
+      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, TypeOK, Messages, Ballots
+    <2>2. Init => MsgInv
+      BY DEF Init, MsgInv
+    <2>3. Init => AccInv
+      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, AccInv, VotedForIn, Ballots
+    <2>4. QED
+      BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3 DEF Inv
+  <1>2. Inv /\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
+    <2>1. Inv /\ UNCHANGED vars => Inv'
+      BY DEF Inv, vars, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+    <2>2. Inv /\ Next => Inv'
+      <3>1. \A b \in Ballots : Inv /\ Phase1a(b) => Inv'
+        <4>1. TAKE b \in Ballots
+        <4>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase1a(b)
+                PROVE Inv'
+          <5>1. TypeOK'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase1a, Send, Messages, Ballots
+          <5>2. MsgInv'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, MsgInv, Phase1a, Send, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+          <5>3. AccInv'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1a, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
+          <5>4. QED
+            BY <5>1, <5>2, <5>3 DEF Inv
+        <4>3. QED
+          BY <4>2
+      <3>2. \A a \in Acceptors : Inv /\ Phase1b(a) => Inv'
+        <4>1. TAKE a \in Acceptors
+        <4>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase1b(a)
+                PROVE Inv'
+          <5>0. PICK mm \in msgs :
+                    /\ mm.type = "1a"
+                    /\ mm.bal > maxBal[a]
+                    /\ maxBal' = [maxBal EXCEPT ![a] = mm.bal]
+                    /\ Send([type |-> "1b", bal |-> mm.bal,
+                          maxVBal |-> maxVBal[a], maxVal |-> maxVal[a], acc |-> a])
+                    /\ UNCHANGED <<maxVBal, maxVal>>
+            BY <4>2 DEF Phase1b
+          <5>1. TypeOK'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0 DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots
+          <5>2. \A v \in Values, b \in Ballots : SafeAt(v, b) => SafeAt(v, b)'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>1, SafeAtStable DEF Next
+          <5>3. \A aa \in Acceptors, v \in Values, bb \in Ballots :
+                    VotedForIn(aa, v, bb)' <=> VotedForIn(aa, v, bb)
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0 DEF Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn
+          <5>4. MsgInv'
+            <6>1. TAKE m \in msgs'
+            <6>2. CASE m \in msgs
+              BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>2, <5>3 DEF Inv, MsgInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+            <6>3. CASE m \notin msgs
+              BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>3, VotedInv, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+            <6>4. QED
+              BY <6>1, <6>2, <6>3 DEF MsgInv
+          <5>5. AccInv'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>3 DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
+          <5>6. QED
+            BY <5>1, <5>4, <5>5 DEF Inv
+        <4>3. QED
+          BY <4>2
+      <3>3. \A b \in Ballots : Inv /\ Phase2a(b) => Inv'
+        <4>1. TAKE b \in Ballots
+        <4>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase2a(b)
+                PROVE Inv'
+          <5>1. TypeOK'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots
+          <5>2. \A v \in Values, b \in Ballots : SafeAt(v, b) => SafeAt(v, b)'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>1, SafeAtStable DEF Next
+          <5>3. \A aa \in Acceptors, v \in Values, bb \in Ballots :
+                    VotedForIn(aa, v, bb)' <=> VotedForIn(aa, v, bb)
+            BY <4>1, <4>2 DEF Phase2a, Send, VotedForIn
+          <5>4. MsgInv'
+            <6>1. TAKE m \in msgs'
+            <6>2. CASE m \in msgs
+              BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>2, <5>3 DEF Inv, MsgInv, Phase2a, Send, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+            <6>3. CASE m \notin msgs
+              BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>2, <5>3, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+            <6>4. QED
+              BY <6>1, <6>2, <6>3 DEF MsgInv
+          <5>5. AccInv'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>3 DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase2a, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
+          <5>6. QED
+            BY <5>1, <5>4, <5>5 DEF Inv
+        <4>3. QED
+          BY <4>2
+      <3>4. \A a \in Acceptors : Inv /\ Phase2b(a) => Inv'
+        <4>1. TAKE a \in Acceptors
+        <4>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase2b(a)
+                PROVE Inv'
+          <5>0. PICK mm \in msgs :
+                    /\ mm.type = "2a"
+                    /\ mm.bal >= maxBal[a]
+                    /\ maxVBal' = [maxVBal EXCEPT ![a] = mm.bal]
+                    /\ maxBal' = [maxBal EXCEPT ![a] = mm.bal]
+                    /\ maxVal' = [maxVal EXCEPT ![a] = mm.val]
+                    /\ Send([type |-> "2b", bal |-> mm.bal, val |-> mm.val, acc |-> a])
+            BY <4>2 DEF Phase2b
+          <5>1. TypeOK'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots
+          <5>2. \A v \in Values, b \in Ballots : SafeAt(v, b) => SafeAt(v, b)'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>1, SafeAtStable DEF Next
+          <5>3. MsgInv'
+            <6>1. TAKE m \in msgs'
+            <6>2. CASE m \in msgs
+              BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>2, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+            <6>3. CASE m \notin msgs
+              BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>2, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+            <6>4. QED
+              BY <6>1, <6>2, <6>3 DEF MsgInv
+          <5>4. AccInv'
+            BY <4>1, <4>2, <5>0, <5>2, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
+          <5>5. QED
+            BY <5>1, <5>3, <5>4 DEF Inv
+        <4>3. QED
+          BY <4>2
+      <3>5. QED
+        BY <3>1, <3>2, <3>3, <3>4 DEF Next
+    <2>3. QED
+      BY <2>1, <2>2 DEF vars
+  <1>3. QED
+    BY <1>1, <1>2, PTL DEF Spec

item.text: I’m going to collapse back to the smaller split and use specific backend pragmas only where necessary.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('THEOREM Invariant ==')
end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
proof=r'''THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv
PROOF
  <1>1. Init => Inv
    <2>1. Init => TypeOK
      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, TypeOK, Messages, Ballots
    <2>2. Init => MsgInv
      BY DEF Init, MsgInv
    <2>3. Init => AccInv
      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, AccInv, VotedForIn, Ballots
    <2>4. QED
      BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3 DEF Inv
  <1>2. Inv /\\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
    <2>1. Inv /\\ UNCHANGED vars => Inv'
      BY DEF Inv, vars, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
    <2>2. Inv /\\ Next => Inv'
      <3>1. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase1a(b) => Inv'
        <4>1. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase1a(b) => TypeOK'
          BY DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase1a, Send, Messages, Ballots
        <4>2. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase1a(b) => MsgInv'
          BY DEF Inv, MsgInv, Phase1a, Send, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
        <4>3. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase1a(b) => AccInv'
          BY DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1a, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
        <4>4. QED
          BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>3 DEF Inv
      <3>2. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => Inv'
        <4>1. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => TypeOK'
          BY DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots
        <4>2. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => MsgInv'
          BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, NoneNotAValue, Z3T(20) DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
        <4>3. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => AccInv'
          BY Z3T(20) DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
        <4>4. QED
          BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>3 DEF Inv
      <3>3. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase2a(b) => Inv'
        <4>1. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase2a(b) => TypeOK'
          BY DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots
        <4>2. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase2a(b) => MsgInv'
          BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue, Z3T(20) DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
        <4>3. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase2a(b) => AccInv'
          BY DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase2a, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
        <4>4. QED
          BY <4>1, <4>2, <4>3 DEF Inv
      <3>4. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase2b(a) => Inv'
        <4>1. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase2b(a) => TypeOK'
          BY NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots
        <4>2. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase2b(a) => MsgInv'
          BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue, Z3T(20) DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt

item.text: I’ll manually prove the simplest failing case (`Phase1b` preserves `AccInv`) by splitting on the updated acceptor.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "apply_patch <<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Paxos_Invariant.tla
@@
         <4>3. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => AccInv'
-          BY Z3T(20) DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
+          <5>1. TAKE a \\in Acceptors
+          <5>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase1b(a)
+                  PROVE AccInv'
+            <6>1. PICK mm \\in msgs :
+                      /\\ mm.type = \"1a\"
+                      /\\ mm.bal > maxBal[a]
+                      /\\ maxBal' = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![a] = mm.bal]
+                      /'"\\ Send([type |-> \"1b\", bal |-> mm.bal,
+                            maxVBal |-> maxVBal[a], maxVal |-> maxVal[a], acc |-> a])
+                      /\\ UNCHANGED <<maxVBal, maxVal>>
+              BY <5>2 DEF Phase1b
+            <6>2. \\A aa \\in Acceptors :
+                      /\\ (maxVal'[aa] = None) <=> (maxVBal'[aa] = -1)
+                      /\\ maxVBal'[aa] =< maxBal'[aa]
+                      /\\ (maxVBal'[aa] >= 0) => VotedForIn(aa, maxVal'[aa], maxVBal'[aa])'
+                      /\\ \\A c \\in Ballots : c > maxVBal'[aa] => ~ \\E v \\in Values : VotedForIn(aa, v, c)'
+              <7>1. TAKE aa \\in Acceptors
+              <7>2. VotedForIn(aa, maxVal'[aa], maxVBal'[aa])' <=> VotedForIn(aa, maxVal[aa], maxVBal[aa])
+                BY <5>2, <6>1 DEF Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn
+              <7>3. \\A c \\in Ballots : (\\E v \\in Values : VotedForIn(aa, v, c)') <=> (\\E v \\in Values : VotedForIn(aa, v, c))
+                BY <5>2, <6>1 DEF Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn
+              <7>4. CASE aa = a
+                BY <5>1, <5>2, <6>1, <7>2, <7>3 DEF Inv, TypeOK, AccInv, Phase1b, Send
+              <7>5. CASE aa # a
+                BY <5>1, <5>2, <6>1, <7>2, <7>3 DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send
+              <7>6. QED
+                BY <7>4, <7>5
+            <6>3. QED
+              BY <6>2 DEF AccInv
*** End Patch
PATCH
python3 check_proof.py Paxos_Invariant.tla"

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text()
# revert manual phase1b accinv block to one line
start=s.index('        <4>3. \\\\A a \\\\in Acceptors : Inv /\\\\ Phase1b(a) => AccInv\\'')
end=s.index('        <4>4. QED', start)
block='''        <4>3. \\\\A a \\\\in Acceptors : Inv /\\\\ Phase1b(a) => AccInv'\\n          BY Force DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots\\n'''
s=s[:start]+block+s[end:]
s=s.replace('Z3T(20) DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2a','Force DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2a')
s=s.replace('Z3T(20) DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2b','Force DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2b')
s=s.replace('Z3T(20) DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2b','Force DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase2b')
p.write_text(s)
PY
python3 check_proof.py Paxos_Invariant.tla"

item.text: I’ll replace the problematic detailed `Phase1b` subproof with the compact form that TLAPS parses reliably.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text()
start=s.index('        <4>3. \\\\A a \\\\in Acceptors : Inv /\\\\ Phase1b(a) => AccInv\\'')
end=s.index('        <4>4. QED', start)
block='''        <4>3. \\\\A a \\\\in Acceptors : Inv /\\\\ Phase1b(a) => AccInv'\\n          BY Z3 DEF Inv, TypeOK, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots\\n'''
s=s[:start]+block+s[end:]
p.write_text(s)
PY
python3 check_proof.py Paxos_Invariant.tla"

item.text: I’ll use `SUFFICES ASSUME` for the quantified action preservation goals, which should produce smaller obligations than nested implication.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text()
# Rebuild proof with SUFFICES for action goals compactly
start=s.index('THEOREM Invariant ==')
end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
proof=r'''THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv
PROOF
  <1>1. Init => Inv
    <2>1. Init => TypeOK
      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, TypeOK, Messages, Ballots
    <2>2. Init => MsgInv
      BY DEF Init, MsgInv
    <2>3. Init => AccInv
      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, AccInv, VotedForIn, Ballots
    <2>4. QED
      BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3 DEF Inv
  <1>2. Inv /\\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
    <2>1. Inv /\\ UNCHANGED vars => Inv'
      BY DEF Inv, vars, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
    <2>2. Inv /\\ Next => Inv'
      <3>1. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase1a(b) => Inv'
        BY DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1a, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
      <3>2. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => Inv'
        <4>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW a \\in Acceptors, Inv, Phase1b(a)
                PROVE Inv'
          BY Zenon
        <4>2. TypeOK'
          BY DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots
        <4>3. MsgInv'
          BY <4>2, SafeAtStable, VotedInv, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
        <4>4. AccInv'
          BY DEF Inv, TypeOK, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
        <4>5. QED
          BY <4>2, <4>3, <4>4 DEF Inv
      <3>3. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase2a(b) => Inv'
        <4>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW b \\in Ballots, Inv, Phase2a(b)
                PROVE Inv'
          BY Zenon
        <4>2. TypeOK'
          BY DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots
        <4>3. MsgInv'
          BY <4>2, SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
        <4>4. AccInv'
          BY DEF Inv, AccInv, Phase2a, Send, VotedForIn, Ballots
        <4>5. QED
          BY <4>2, <4>3, <4>4 DEF Inv
      <3>4. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase2b(a) => Inv'
        <4>1. SUFFICES ASSUME NEW a \\in Acceptors, Inv, Phase2b(a)
                PROVE Inv'
          BY Zenon
        <4>2. TypeOK'
          BY NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots
        <4>3. MsgInv'
          BY <4>2, SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
        <4>4. AccInv'
          BY <4>2, SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
        <4>5. QED
          BY <4>2, <4>3, <4>4 DEF Inv
      <3>5. QED
        BY <3>1, <3>2, <3>3, <3>4 DEF Next
    <2>3. QED
      BY <2>1, <2>2 DEF vars
  <1>3. QED
    BY <1>1, <1>2, PTL DEF Spec
'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
python3 check_proof.py Paxos_Invariant.tla"

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text()
old='''        <4>2. \\\\A a \\\\in Acceptors : Inv /\\\\ Phase1b(a) => MsgInv'\\n          BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt\\n'''
new='''        <4>2. \\\\A a \\\\in Acceptors : Inv /\\\\ Phase1b(a) => MsgInv'\\n          <5>1. TAKE a \\\\in Acceptors\\n          <5>2. ASSUME Inv, Phase1b(a)\\n                  PROVE MsgInv'\\n            <6>1. PICK p \\\\in msgs :\\n                      /\\\\ p.type = \"1a\"\\n                      /\\\\ p.bal > maxBal[a]\\n                      /\\\\ maxBal' = [maxBal EXCEPT "'![a] = p.bal]'"\\n                      /\\\\ msgs' = msgs \\\\cup {[type |-> \"1b\", bal |-> p.bal,\\n                           maxVBal |-> maxVBal[a], maxVal |-> maxVal[a], acc |-> a]}\\n                      /\\\\ maxVBal' = maxVBal\\n                      /\\\\ maxVal' = maxVal\\n              BY <5>2 DEF Phase1b, Send\\n            <6>2. TypeOK'\\n              BY <5>1, <5>2, <6>1 DEF Inv, TypeOK, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots\\n            <6>3. \\\\A v \\\\in Values, b \\\\in Ballots : SafeAt(v, b) => SafeAt(v, b)'\\n              BY <5>2, <6>2, SafeAtStable DEF Inv, Next, Phase1b\\n            <6>4. TAKE m \\\\in msgs'\\n            <6>5. CASE m \\\\in msgs\\n              BY <5>2, <6>1, <6>3 DEF Inv, MsgInv, Phase1b, Send, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt\\n            <6>6. CASE m = [type |-> \"1b\", bal |-> p.bal,\\n                           maxVBal |-> maxVBal[a], maxVal |-> maxVal[a], acc |-> a]\\n              BY <5>1, <5>2, <6>1, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, TypeOK, AccInv, MsgInv, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt\\n            <6>7. QED\\n              BY <6>1, <6>4, <6>5, <6>6 DEF MsgInv\\n          <5>3. QED\\n            BY <5>2\\n'''
if old not in s:
    raise SystemExit('old not found')
p.write_text(s.replace(old,new))
PY

item.text: I’m testing a smaller temporal proof approach using TLAPS’s own temporal prover rather than action expansion.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text(); start=s.index('THEOREM Invariant =='); end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
proof='''THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv\\nPROOF\\n  <1>1. Init => Inv\\n    BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn\\n  <1>2. Inv /\\\\ [Next]_vars => Inv\\'\\n    BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, vars, Next, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1a, Phase1b, Phase2a, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt\\n  <1>3. QED\\n    BY <1>1, <1>2, LS4 DEF Spec\\n'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm --cleanfp --line 171 -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps Paxos_Invariant.tla"

item.text: I’ll give the SMT backend more time explicitly on the remaining hard preservation facts.

Inv /\\\\ [Next]_vars => Inv\\'\\n    BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue DEF Inv, vars, Next, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1a, Phase1b, Phase2a, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt\\n  <1>3. QED\\n    BY <1>1, <1>2, LS4 DEF Spec\\n'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm --cleanfp --line 171 -I /opt/tlapm15/lib/tlaps Paxos_Invariant.tla"

item.text: I’m checking the local proof checker’s `tlapm` discovery logic; maybe it’s useful for diagnosing why direct and checker runs differ.

item.command: /bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,120p' check_proof.py"

item.aggregated_output:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Check a single TLAPS benchmark proof for correctness and cheating.

Designed for AI agents to get quick feedback during proof writing.

Usage:
    python3 check_proof.py benchmark/Euclid/GCD_GCD3.tla [--tlapm PATH] [--tlapm-lib PATH] [--timeout SECS]

Output:
    - Raw tlapm output (preserved verbatim)
    - Cheating check (compares against main branch)
    - Summary verdict: PASS / FAIL / CHEATING

Exit codes:
    0 = PASS
    1 = FAIL (proof doesn't verify)
    2 = CHEATING (tampering detected)
    3 = ERROR (could not run check)
"""

import os
import sys
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import tempfile
import argparse
import glob

from cheating_detection import (
    detect_proof_omitted, detect_extra_axioms,
    detect_preamble_modification, detect_empty_proof,
    detect_zero_total_obligations, detect_dependency_modification,
    strip_comments,
)


def find_tlapm():
    """Find tlapm binary."""
    for candidate in ['/opt/tlapm15/bin/tlapm', '/tmp/tlapm15/bin/tlapm', '/tmp/tlapm/bin/tlapm', shutil.which('tlapm')]:
        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
            return candidate
    return None


def find_tlapm_lib(tlapm_path):
    """Derive lib path from tlapm binary path."""
    base = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(tlapm_path))
    for sub in ['lib/tlaps', 'lib/tlapm', 'lib']:
        path = os.path.join(base, sub)
        if os.path.isdir(path):
            return path
    return None


def get_main_version(filepath):
    """Get the file content from the main/master branch."""
    repo_root = subprocess.run(
        ['git', 'rev-parse', '--show-toplevel'],
        capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=os.path.dirname(filepath) or '.'
    ).stdout.strip()

    if not repo_root:
        return None

    rel_path = os.path.relpath(filepath, repo_root)

    for branch in ['main', 'master']:
        result = subprocess.run(
            ['git', 'show', f'{branch}:{rel_path}'],
            capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=repo_root
        )
        if result.returncode == 0:
            return result.stdout

    return None


def find_proof_obvious_line(lines):
    """Find the line number (0-indexed) of 'PROOF OBVIOUS' in the file."""
    for i in range(len(lines) - 1, -1, -1):
        if lines[i].strip() == 'PROOF OBVIOUS':
            return i
    return None


def check_cheating(filepath):
    """Check for cheating by comparing against main branch.

    Returns list of (line_number_1indexed, description) issues.
    """
    issues = []

    # Get main branch version
    main_content = get_main_version(filepath)
    if main_content is None:
        issues.append((0, "WARNING: Could not retrieve main branch version for comparison"))
        return issues

    main_lines = main_content.split('\n')
    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
        current_content = f.read()
    current_lines = current_content.split('\n')

    # Find PROOF OBVIOUS in main version - this is the boundary
    po_line = find_proof_obvious_line(main_lines)
    if po_line is None:
        issues.append((0, "WARNING: No PROOF OBVIOUS found in main branch version"))
        return issues

    if len(current_lines) < po_line:
        issues.append((len(current_lines), f"File truncated: preamble had {po_line} lines, file has {len(current_lines)}"))
        return issues

    # 1. Preamble modification (shared module)
    for ci in detect_preamble_modification(main_lines, current_lines, po_line):
        issues.append((ci.line or 0, ci.description))

    # 2. PROOF OMITTED / bare OMITTED in proof section (shared module)

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('Paxos_Invariant.tla')
s=p.read_text(); start=s.index('THEOREM Invariant =='); end=s.index('\\n=============================================================================', start)
proof=r'''THEOREM Invariant == Spec => []Inv
PROOF
  <1>1. Init => Inv
    <2>1. Init => TypeOK
      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, TypeOK, Messages, Ballots
    <2>2. Init => MsgInv
      BY DEF Init, MsgInv
    <2>3. Init => AccInv
      BY NoneNotAValue DEF Init, AccInv, VotedForIn, Ballots
    <2>4. QED
      BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3 DEF Inv
  <1>2. Inv /\\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
    <2>1. Inv /\\ UNCHANGED vars => Inv'
      BY DEF Inv, vars, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
    <2>2. Inv /\\ Next => Inv'
      <3>1. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase1a(b) => Inv'
        BY SimplifyAndSolve DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Phase1a, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
      <3>2. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase1b(a) => Inv'
        BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, NoneNotAValue, SlowSimplifyAndSolve DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase1b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
      <3>3. \\A b \\in Ballots : Inv /\\ Phase2a(b) => Inv'
        BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue, SlowSimplifyAndSolve DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase2a, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
      <3>4. \\A a \\in Acceptors : Inv /\\ Phase2b(a) => Inv'
        BY SafeAtStable, VotedInv, VotedOnce, NoneNotAValue, SlowSimplifyAndSolve DEF Inv, TypeOK, MsgInv, AccInv, Next, Phase2b, Send, Messages, Ballots, VotedForIn, WontVoteIn, SafeAt
      <3>5. QED
        BY <3>1, <3>2, <3>3, <3>4 DEF Next
    <2>3. QED
      BY <2>1, <2>2 DEF vars
  <1>3. QED
    BY <1>1, <1>2, PTL DEF Spec
'''
p.write_text(s[:start]+proof+s[end:])
PY
python3 check_proof.py Paxos_Invariant.tla --timeout 300"

item.text:
I’m sorry, but I wasn’t able to get `Paxos_Invariant.tla` to a passing `check_proof.py` state within this run.

I replaced the placeholder with several TLAPS proof attempts and verified that the cheating checker itself stays clean, but the final theorem still has TLAPS backend failures on the action-preservation obligations, especially the `Phase1b`/`Phase2a`/`Phase2b` invariant preservation cases. The latest `python3 check_proof.py Paxos_Invariant.tla --timeout 300` still reports failure, so I can’t honestly claim the proof is complete.
